behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between PR-change detection and analysis scheduling, not just a single local algorithm. A unit test of a filter function can pass while wiring still feeds unchanged files. Higher levels are not required by behavior text because this can be caught with in-process modules and a fake changed-file source (no required real network/OS/subprocess).
behaviors.test_description: Wire the changed-file collector module to the analysis dispatcher with a fixture repo/file set where only a subset is marked changed; assert analyzer is invoked only for changed paths and never for unchanged paths.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: The real failure is commonly at module boundaries: analyzer output -> grouping/formatting -> comment publisher payload. Unit tests of grouping alone may miss serialization/order/section wiring defects. Rejected System because behavior does not require real GitHub HTTP to validate grouping correctness in posted content; a fake comment client capturing payload is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Run analyzer-result aggregation through the comment-construction path and send via a fake PR comment client; assert resulting comment body contains distinct error/warning/info groups with correct finding membership.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: This is an environment/runtime SLO tied to GitHub Actions timeout behavior. In-process mocks can hide real subprocess startup cost, filesystem throughput, and runner constraints. Rejected Integration because behavior explicitly targets completion under real Actions timeout conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the lint step end-to-end in a CI-like run (same invocation path as GitHub Action) against a PR fixture with 200 changed files; measure wall-clock completion and assert it stays below 10 minutes.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text says 'linter process crashes', which is a real subprocess failure boundary (exit signal/non-zero termination). Mocked exceptions can miss actual process-crash semantics and restart/continue logic. Rejected Integration due explicit subprocess-crash requirement in the behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run analysis with a test linter executable (or real linter configured to crash on a malformed fixture file) that crashes for one file; assert pipeline continues processing subsequent files and publishes a final PR-comment payload containing remaining findings plus crash reporting.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is local dedup logic (keying by file/line/rule fingerprint). No required module wiring, OS, network, subprocess, or model output dependency is implied. Rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on dedup rule outcome, which can be fully exercised with direct in-memory findings input.
behaviors.test_description: Call the dedup function/object with a findings list containing intentional duplicates from overlapping rules on the same file/line and assert output retains one canonical finding per dedup key.
